description_long: This town hall is a large, L-shaped timber-framed building with a steep, dark green shingled roof and prominent gables. The facade features a series of large, arched windows with wooden latticework, and the upper story is supported by visible wooden beams and posts. Stone chimneys rise from the roof, and wooden ladders are attached to the exterior, suggesting access to upper levels or maintenance areas. The lower level is constructed from horizontal wooden planks, while the upper level showcases decorative timber framing and overhanging eaves. The building exudes a rustic, medieval or fantasy-inspired architectural style, with warm lighting visible through the windows, hinting at an inviting interior. No signage or flags are visible, and the entrance is not clearly shown but is likely located at the central section of the facade.
description: A large, L-shaped timber-framed town hall with a steep green shingled roof, arched windows, and decorative wooden beams, evoking a rustic medieval style.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main facade with three prominent gables, large arched windows, and decorative timber framing.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the L-shaped structure, visible stone chimney, and wooden ladder attached to the exterior.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full length of the building, stone chimneys, and the extensive shingled roof with overhanging eaves.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the secondary facade, another wooden ladder, and the continuation of the timber framing and window design.
attribute_architectural_style: Medieval Revival with rustic timber framing
attribute_facade_ornamentation: Decorative timber framing, arched windows with latticework, overhanging eaves, and exposed wooden beams
attribute_entrance_design: Main entrance likely features a single or double wooden door beneath the central gable, framed by timber posts and possibly steps, but not clearly visible in the images
